title: rubus idaeus subsp. melanolasius
intro: Rubus idaeus subsp. melanolasius, also known as black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus idaeus species, characterized by its dark purple color and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of black raspberry include its high content of anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of black raspberry include its ability to protect against cancer, heart disease, and cognitive decline, as well as its potential to support digestive health. Black raspberry is commonly prepared as a juice, tea, or supplement, and can also be used in herbal remedies and culinary dishes.
